digest:
# Daily Macro Digest — 19 July 2026

**Summary:** The headline story is a sharp ~4.5% jump in both WTI and Brent crude, alongside a 12% spike in the VIX (volatility index) — a classic sign of markets pricing in more uncertainty. Gold and silver ticked up modestly, the US dollar and 10-year yield were little changed, and AUD/USD slipped slightly. The standout local mover was the ASX materials basket (BHP, RIO, FMG, NST), down 2.3%. Unfortunately most news feeds failed to load today (403 errors), so the causal picture is thinner than usual — commentary pieces from oil trade press reference "mounting risks" and fading "illusion of oil abundance," consistent with supply-side jitters, but no confirmed trigger event (e.g., a specific Hormuz incident) came through in the available headlines.

**VIX (+12.2%, anomaly)**
This is an unusually large jump, suggesting a genuine risk-off shift — money moving away from risk assets toward safety. Under the usual pattern (relationship #7), this typically comes with gold up, equities under pressure, and AUD softer, which broadly matches today (gold +0.68%, AUD -0.21%). USD, however, is flat rather than stronger, so the risk-off signal isn't showing up everywhere. *Historical pattern context: our archive
